content: Anti-pattern (occurred TWICE, two independent agents): bulk guest-count rewrites that replace every flagged integer with the page's canonical also rewrite integers inside cross-ship COMPARISON sentences, producing factually false pages. Example: queen-anne.html 'Queen Elizabeth or Queen Victoria may be a better fit at roughly 90,000 GT and 2,081 guests' was rewritten to 2,996 (Queen Anne's own count) — the page then claimed Elizabeth carries Anne's capacity. Fix shipped 2026-07-11: COMPARISON_CONTEXT_TRIGGERS (if-you-want/prefer idioms, 'may be a better fit', '<Class> class carries/has', 'intimate ocean ships at under', by-contrast/unlike) in BOTH admin/repair-internal-consistency.js and validate-ship-page.js validateInternalNumericConsistency, with 200-char lookback + 100-char lookahead. Validator and repair script must share the same trigger list or they fight each other. Rule doc: .claude/skills/internal-consistency-repair/SKILL.md § 3a. Revert audit: audit-reports/internal-consistency/_revert-2026-05-07.md.
